title: salix bangongensis
intro: Salix bangongensis, also known as the Tibetan willow, is a species of willow tree native to the high-altitude regions of the Tibetan Plateau. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Salix bangongensis include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Salix bangongensis is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or salves, and is also used as a natural dye.
